label: Heel contusion without fracture or diabetic-foot red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit a heel bruise.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a heel bruise. We did not find signs of fracture, poor circulation, infection, or a diabetic-foot emergency today.
home: Use ice wrapped in a cloth for short periods.
Raise the foot when you can.
meds: Use acetaminophen or ibuprofen only if you can take it safely and follow the label.
Use medicines only as prescribed or recommended.
return: Worsening pain, new numbness, blue or pale toes, or inability to walk.
Spreading redness, fever, pus, or a new wound.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, podiatry, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated heel contusion.
No fracture concern, diabetic-foot pathway, infection, wound, or neurovascular compromise.
exclusion: Fracture or dislocation concern, open wound, diabetic foot, peripheral vascular disease, neuropathy, infection, high-energy trauma, or specialist-directed podiatry plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus bruise material supports contusion self-care, expected color change, pain control, and escalation for worsening symptoms or concerning swelling. This autonomous expansion limits the phenotype to clinician-diagnosed heel contusion with fracture, diabetic-foot, infection, wound, and neurovascular red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of heel contusion

label: Palm contusion without fracture or tendon red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit a palm bruise.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a palm bruise. We did not find signs of fracture, tendon injury, infection, or poor blood flow today.
home: Use ice wrapped in a cloth for short periods.
Keep the hand elevated when swelling is present.
meds: Use acetaminophen or ibuprofen only if you can take it safely and follow the label.
Use medicines only as prescribed or recommended.
return: New numbness, weakness, blue or pale fingers, or trouble moving the fingers.
Worsening pain, swelling, spreading redness, pus, or fever.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, hand clinic, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated palm contusion.
No fracture concern, tendon injury, open wound, infection, or neurovascular compromise.
exclusion: Fracture or dislocation concern, tendon injury, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, or specialist-directed hand plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus bruise material supports local contusion home care and escalation for worsening pain, swelling, or concerning symptoms. This expansion keeps palm contusion separate from hand fracture, tendon injury, wound, infection, and specialist-directed pathways.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of palm contusion

label: Upper arm strain without rupture or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit an upper arm strain.
found: Your clinician diagnosed an upper arm strain. We did not find signs of fracture, tendon rupture, infection, or poor blood flow today.
home: Use ice wrapped in a cloth for short periods.
Avoid heavy lifting until pain is improving.
meds: Use acetaminophen or ibuprofen only if you can take it safely and follow the label.
Use medicines only as prescribed or recommended.
return: New numbness, weakness, blue or pale fingers, or severe worsening pain.
New deformity, inability to use the arm, fever, redness, or swelling.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, orthopedics, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated upper arm strain.
No fracture concern, tendon rupture concern, infection, or neurovascular compromise.
exclusion: Fracture or dislocation concern, tendon rupture concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus sprain and strain material supports rest, symptom care, gradual return, and escalation for worsening pain, swelling, numbness, or inability to move. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed upper arm strain with rupture, fracture, infection, and neurovascular red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of upper arm strain

label: Ankle tendinitis without rupture or infection red flags
found: Your clinician diagnosed ankle tendinitis. We did not find signs of fracture, tendon rupture, infection, or poor blood flow today.
home: Use ice wrapped in a cloth for short periods.
Return to activity gradually as pain improves.
meds: Use acetaminophen or ibuprofen only if you can take it safely and follow the label.
Use medicines only as prescribed or recommended.
return: New numbness, blue or pale toes, inability to walk, or severe worsening pain.
Fever, spreading redness, major swelling, or concern for infection.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, orthopedics, podiatry, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated ankle tendinitis.
No fracture concern, tendon rupture concern, infection, or neurovascular compromise.
exclusion: Fracture or dislocation concern, tendon rupture concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, inability to bear weight, diabetic-foot pathway, or specialist-directed orthopedic/podiatry plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus tendinitis material supports tendon inflammation framing, rest/activity modification, symptom care, and escalation for worsening pain or functional problems. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed ankle tendinitis with rupture, fracture, infection, diabetic-foot, and neurovascular red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of ankle tendinitis
